title: 10 best herbal essential oils for flu
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for flu are concentrated plant extracts that help alleviate flu symptoms by boosting the immune system and reducing inflammation. The best essential oils for flu include Eucalyptus globulus, Echinacea purpurea, Melaleuca alternifolia, Zingiber officinale, Thymus vulgaris, Rosmarinus officinalis, Ginkgo biloba, Cinnamomum verum, Glycyrrhiza glabra, and Echinacea angustifolia. If left untreated, flu can lead to prolonged illness, missed work or school, and even hospitalization, negatively impacting daily life, relationships, and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower essential oils helps with flu because of its remarkable antimicrobial properties. The oil contains compounds such as echinacoside, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of influenza viruses, reducing their ability to replicate and spread. Additionally, purple coneflower essential oils has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms such as congestion and sore throats. By inhaling or applying topically, it can provide natural relief from flu symptoms and promote a faster recovery.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Purple coneflower essential oil has been shown to reduce inflammation, which is a common symptom of the flu and can help alleviate body aches and pains.
Antiviral properties: The oil has been found to have antiviral activity against several strains of influenza virus, making it a potential natural remedy for helping to prevent or treat the flu.

Immune-boosting properties: Purple coneflower essential oil contains compounds that have been shown to stimulate the immune system, which can help the body fight off infection and reduce the severity and duration of flu symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots are often used to create essential oils that help alleviate flu symptoms such as fever and body aches.
Seeds: Containing volatile oils and fatty acids, seeds contribute to the production of essential oils that aid in boosting immunity and reducing inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves from your garden or purchase from a reputable supplier.
Dry the echinacea mixture in a warm oven set at 180°f for 4-6 hours or air-dry it for several days.
Steam distill the dried echinacea material using a copper still and collecting the essential oil in a glass container.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove any impurities and sediment.
Bottle the clear echinacea purpurea essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and store it in a cool, dry place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree essential oils helps with flu because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which help to combat the underlying causes of the flu. The oil's antiviral compounds have been shown to effectively inhibit the replication of viruses, including the influenza virus. Additionally, its expectorant properties can help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion. This natural remedy can provide relief from flu symptoms and support the body's recovery process.
remedy_properties: Antiviral activity: The essential oil of Ginkgo biloba has been shown to exhibit antiviral properties against influenza A and B viruses, which can help alleviate symptoms of the flu.
Anti-inflammatory effects: The oil's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce fever, body aches, and respiratory congestion associated with flu.
Immune system support: Ginkgo biloba essential oil has been found to stimulate immune function by increasing the production of interferon, an antiviral protein that helps fight off viral infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing anti-inflammatory compounds, the roots help reduce fever and alleviate body aches associated with the flu.
Barks: Rich in antioxidants, the barks help combat oxidative stress caused by the flu virus, promoting overall health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves from mature trees in autumn or spring.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator on low setting for 12 hours.
Grind 20 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves into fine powder using mortar and pestle.
Combine 50 ml carrier oil like coconut or olive oil with 1 gram powdered ginkgo biloba in dark glass bottle.
Store mixture at room temperature for 2 weeks shaking daily then strain and filter the resulting essential oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum essential oils helps with flu because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which can help combat the influenza virus. The oil's eucalyptol content also acts as a natural decongestant, relieving congestion and sinus pressure associated with flu symptoms. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce body aches and fever, making it an effective natural remedy to alleviate flu discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum essential oil has been shown to inhibit the replication of influenza viruses, reducing the severity and duration of flu symptoms.
Decongestant and expectorant properties: The oil's menthol content helps to relieve congestion and reduce mucus production, making it easier to breathe and expel phlegm during flu infection.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum essential oil has anti-inflammatory compounds that can help alleviate symptoms such as headaches, body aches, and fever associated with the flu.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Tasmanian Blue Gum contain sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of the flu.
Barks: The barks of the Tasmanian Blue Gum are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which possess antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, making them effective in combating influenza.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality eucalyptus globulus leaves in the early morning for optimal oil content and flavor.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6-8 hours or until completely dry.
Combine dried eucalyptus globulus leaves with carrier oil such as coconut oil at a ratio of 1:4.
Steam distill the mixture in a still for 2-3 hours, collecting the essential oils that separate from the water.
Filter the collected essential oil using a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove impurities and sediment before storing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme essential oils helps with flu because of its antimicrobial properties, which combat the viral infections that cause the flu. The oil contains compounds like thymol and carvacrol, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of influenza virus and other pathogens. When inhaled or applied topically, thyme essential oil may help reduce the severity and duration of flu symptoms, such as congestion, coughing, and sore throats. Additionally, its decongestant properties can help alleviate sinus pressure and relieve aches and pains.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Thyme oil's antiviral compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, help combat influenza viruses by disrupting their replication and preventing them from multiplying.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme oil's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, reduce swelling and pain in the respiratory tract, alleviating symptoms like congestion, coughing, and sore throats.
Expectorant properties: Thyme oil's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to expel and reducing the severity of coughs.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the thyme plant contain a higher concentration of thymol, a compound that has been shown to have antiviral properties, making them effective in fighting off influenza.
Leaves: Thyme leaves are rich in cineole and other bioactive compounds that can help alleviate flu symptoms such as congestion, coughing, and sore throats.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and stems in late summer or early fall.
Dry the plant material using a dehydrator or air drying method for 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried plant material using water at 100°c for 3 hours.
Filter the essential oil from the distillation process using activated charcoal filter paper.
Bottle the essential oil in dark glass containers and store it in a cool place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold essential oils helps with flu because of its potent antimicrobial properties that combat influenza viruses. The oil's anti-inflammatory compounds soothe congestion and reduce fever, alleviating symptoms such as coughing, sore throats, and body aches. Additionally, its expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe. By inhaling or applying topically, pot marigold essential oil can provide relief from flu symptoms and promote recovery. Its natural benefits make it an effective and holistic way to combat the flu virus.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Pot marigold essential oil has antiviral properties that can help combat the virus that causes the flu, reducing its severity and duration.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help alleviate symptoms such as body aches, headache, and sore throat associated with the flu.
Immune system support: Pot marigold essential oil has been traditionally used to support the immune system, helping to boost its function and reduce the risk of contracting illnesses like the flu.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing a rich concentration of antioxidants and flavonoids, root extracts help boost immunity and combat viral infections like the flu.
Flowers: The vibrant flowers of pot marigold are rich in carotenoid pigments that have been shown to exhibit antiviral properties, making them an effective ingredient for flu relief essential oils.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried calendula flowers at peak potency for optimal antimicrobial properties.
Combine one cup of dried calendula flowers with two cups of carrier oil such as jojoba or sweet almond oil.
Steam distill the mixture in a stainless steel apparatus at 100-150 degrees fahrenheit for three to four hours.
Filter and decant the essential oil using a coffee filter or cheesecloth within one hour of completion.
Store the calendula officinalis essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder essential oils helps with flu because of its antiviral properties, which can help combat the influenza virus and reduce its severity. The oil's antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory compounds, such as alpha-terpenyl acetate and beta-caryophyllene, work to soothe a sore throat, ease congestion, and reduce fever. Additionally, elder essential oil has expectorant properties, which can help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe and recover from the flu.
remedy_properties: Antiviral: Elderberry oil contains antiviral compounds like anthocyanins, which have been shown to inhibit the replication of influenza viruses, helping to prevent the spread of infection.

Anti-inflammatory: The flavonoids and terpenes in elderberry oil have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling, pain, and inflammation associated with flu symptoms like sinusitis, bronchitis, and pneumonia.

Immune system support: Elderberry oil's antioxidant and antimicrobial properties can help boost the immune system by activating natural killer cells, increasing antibody production, and fighting off invading pathogens.
remedy_parts: Roots: Elder roots have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, making them effective in reducing fever and congestion associated with flu.
Barks: The barks of the elder tree contain flavonoids, which help to soothe a sore throat and reduce inflammation, making it an excellent addition to essential oils for flu relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh sambucus nigra flowers and leaves in early summer morning when dew is still present.
Dry the harvested material in shade for 7-10 days or until it reaches 10% moisture content using paper bags.
Steam distill the dried sambucus nigra material with a ratio of 1:4 water to herb for 2 hours at 100°c temperature.
Collect the resulting essential oil and filter it through coffee filter paper to remove any impurities within 30 minutes.
Store the filtered essential oil in dark glass bottle with tight-fitting lid in cool place away from direct sunlight for up to 12 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wintergreen essential oils helps with flu because of its powerful anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. When used topically or inhaled, it can help reduce fever and alleviate symptoms such as congestion and coughing. The menthol content in wintergreen oil also helps to thin mucus, making it easier to expel from the body. Additionally, its expectorant properties help to clear bronchial passages, promoting easy breathing and relieving chest congestion. Overall, using herbal wintergreen essential oils can provide natural relief from flu symptoms and support the body's recovery process.
remedy_properties: Analgesic and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Wintergreen essential oil's methyl salicylate content helps to reduce pain, fever, and inflammation associated with flu symptoms, making it an effective natural remedy for relieving headaches, body aches, and sore throats.

Antiviral Properties: Wintergreen essential oil has been shown to exhibit antiviral activity against certain viruses, including the influenza virus, which can help to prevent the replication of the virus and reduce the severity of flu symptoms.

Expectorant Properties: The menthol content in wintergreen essential oil helps to thin mucus, making it easier to expel from the lungs and relieve congestion, coughing, and chest tightness associated with flu.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of wintergreen contain high amounts of methyl salicylate, which has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in relieving symptoms of the flu such as sore throats and body aches.
Rhyzomes: Wintergreen rhyzomes are rich in essential oils that have natural antiviral properties, which can help combat the influenza virus and reduce its severity.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh gaultheria procumbens leaves and stems for steam distillation within 30 minutes of collection.
Wash the harvested material in cold running water to remove dirt and debris for 10 minutes.
Steam distill the washed plant material using a steam distillation apparatus at 100°c for 60 minutes.
Collect the essential oil from the condenser coil after distillation and filter it through a coffee filter paper for 15 minutes.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree essential oils helps with flu because of its antiviral properties that help combat the infection-causing viruses. The oil contains compounds like eucalyptol, which helps to relieve congestion and open up airways, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, tea tree oil has antimicrobial properties that aid in fighting off secondary bacterial infections that often develop during flu season. By incorporating tea tree essential oils into your daily routine through steam inhalation or topical application, you can help boost your immune system and reduce the severity of flu symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: Tea Tree Oil has potent antimicrobial properties that can help combat bacterial infections, such as sinusitis or bronchitis, which may accompany the flu.
Anti-inflammatory: The oil's anti-inflammatory effects can help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, throat, and lungs, making it easier to breathe and manage flu symptoms.

Antiviral: While Tea Tree Oil is not effective against viral infections like the flu virus itself, its antiviral properties may help combat secondary bacterial infections that can occur as a result of the flu.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Abundant in cineole, a key component that helps to reduce fever and ease congestion associated with the flu.
Seeds: Containing a high amount of terpinen-4-ol, an antiviral compound that targets influenza viruses and reduces their replication.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of fresh melaleuca alternifolia leaves from the wild or a reputable supplier.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm oven at 30°c for 24 hours or until they crumble easily.
Steam distill the dried leaves using a still and a solvent like water to extract the essential oil.
Collect the distilled oil and filter it through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove impurities.
Bottle the final product in dark glass containers and store it at room temperature for up to 12 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with flu because it possesses anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce fever, congestion, and body aches. Its soothing aroma can also ease stress and anxiety, common symptoms associated with flu. Additionally, its natural decongestant properties can help relieve sinus pressure and promote easy breathing. The calming effects of English lavender essential oils can even aid in promoting better sleep quality, which is often disrupted during the illness.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: English Lavender essential oil has been shown to inhibit the growth of certain viruses, including those responsible for the common cold and flu, helping to prevent the spread of infection.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in English Lavender essential oil can help reduce inflammation in the body, which can alleviate symptoms such as aches and pains associated with the flu.

Decongestant and expectorant properties: English Lavender essential oil has natural decongestant and expectorant properties, helping to clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving congestion caused by the flu.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms such as fever and congestion associated with flu.
Leaves: Lavender leaves contain camphor and linalool, which have antiviral properties that can help combat the influenza virus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavender flowers at dawn when they are fully bloomed and fragrant containing 20% of its total mass.
Dry the freshly harvested lavender flowers in a warm oven at 40°c for 24 hours to remove excess moisture slowly.
Crush the dried lavender flowers into fine powder using a mortar and pestle to release their essential oils gently.
Mix one part lavender powder with two parts carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a clean glass jar within 10 minutes.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for four weeks allowing the essential oils to infuse fully.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curry plant essential oils helps with flu because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which aid in fighting off viral infections. The oil's active compounds, such as curcuminoids and sesquiterpenes, have been shown to inhibit the growth of influenza viruses, reducing their ability to replicate and cause harm. Additionally, curry plant essential oil's decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties help relieve congestion and alleviate symptoms like fever and body aches, making it an effective natural remedy for flu relief.

Anti-inflammatory: The oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help alleviate symptoms such as fever, body aches, and congestion associated with the flu, by reducing inflammation in the body.

Immune-stimulating: Curry Plant Essential Oil has immunomodulatory effects, which means it can enhance the body's natural defense mechanisms, boosting the immune system to fight off infections like the flu.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used to create a natural anti-inflammatory and antiviral oil that helps reduce fever and congestion associated with flu.
Barks: Contains compounds like curcumin and turmeric, which have potent antimicrobial properties that aid in fighting off viral infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh helichrysum italicum flowers at dawn on a dry day with scissors for optimal potency.
Wash the harvested flowers in cold running water and pat them dry with paper towels for thorough cleaning.
Fill a clean glass jar with 200g of the dried helichrysum italicum flowers and add 400ml of high-proof vodka for infusion.
Store the jar in a dark place at room temperature for 2 weeks, shaking it daily for even extraction.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into another clean glass bottle and discard the solids after 30 minutes of pressing for clear oil production.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat flu with herbal essential oils is to combine certain blends of oils with a carrier oil for massage or inhalation. Eucalyptus oil helps ease congestion and coughs, while peppermint oil soothes the throat. Lemon balm oil reduces fever and stress, while ginger oil aids in digestion and relieves nausea. Blend these oils with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil and apply topically to the chest, back, and feet for effective relief from flu symptoms.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to flu that are treated with herbal essential oils are bronchitis, sinusitis, and common colds. Essential oils such as eucalyptus, peppermint, and tea tree oil help alleviate congestion, cough, and fever symptoms. Eucalyptus oil's decongestant properties make it effective against bronchial issues, while peppermint oil's cooling effect soothes a sore throat and sinuses. Tea tree oil's antimicrobial properties combat infection-causing bacteria and viruses.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus essential oils helps with flu because it has decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help relieve congestion and ease coughs associated with the flu. It contains compounds like eucalyptol, which can help break down mucus and open up airways, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties may help combat viral infections by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that can exacerbate flu symptoms, promoting a faster recovery.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): This constituent helps relieve flu symptoms by acting as a bronchodilator, reducing congestion and inflammation in the airways.
Pinene: This terpene constituent helps alleviate flu symptoms by reducing inflammation, relieving coughs, and acting as an expectorant to help clear mucus from the lungs.
Alpha-pinene: This terpene constituent helps with flu relief by acting as an anti-inflammatory, reducing fever, and relieving headaches associated with flu.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used to make essential oils for flu because they are rich in eucalyptol, a compound that helps relieve respiratory issues.
Buds: The buds are used to make essential oils for flu because they have a high concentration of eucalyptol and other compounds that aid in decongesting the airways.
Stems: The stems are used to make essential oils for flu because they contain a mixture of compounds, including eucalyptol, that help reduce inflammation and congestion.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea essential oils helps with flu because it contains bioactive compounds that stimulate the immune system, promoting the production of white blood cells. This increased immune response enables the body to fight off viral infections, such as the flu, more effectively. The essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce congestion and fever, making it a natural remedy for flu symptoms. remedy_constituents: Alkylamides: These constituents help modulate the immune system, reducing the severity and duration of flu symptoms by enhancing the body's natural defense against viral infections.
Cichoric acid: This phenolic compound exhibits antiviral properties, inhibiting the replication of the influenza virus and reducing the severity of flu symptoms.

Icariin: This flavonoid constituent has been shown to have immunomodulatory effects, increasing the production of white blood cells and activating natural killer cells to fight off the flu virus and other pathogens.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are used to make essential oils because they contain the highest concentration of the active compounds responsible for Echinacea's immunomodulatory and antiviral properties.

Roots: The roots are used to make essential oils because they contain a higher concentration of inulin, a polysaccharide with immunomodulatory effects that help to boost the body's defenses against flu viruses.

Leaves: The leaves are used to make essential oils because they contain a range of volatile compounds with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce the severity and duration of flu symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of echinacea purpurea flowers at peak bloom to ensure optimal oil yield and quality.
Dry the harvested flowers in a well-ventilated area for 2-3 days to prevent mold growth.
Steam distill the dried echinacea flowers in a 100:1 ratio with distilled water for 2 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil and filter it using a coffee filter paper to remove impurities.
Store the final echinacea essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils helps with flu because of its potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's active compound, cineole, has been shown to combat viral infections, including the flu virus. It also helps to reduce congestion and alleviate respiratory issues, such as coughs and sore throats. Additionally, the oil's decongestant properties help to clear mucus from the lungs, relieving symptoms of the flu and promoting a faster recovery. remedy_constituents: Cineole (also known as Eucalyptol): Helps with flu by acting as an expectorant, loosening and removing mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe.
Terpinen-4-ol: Contributes to its antiviral properties by inhibiting the replication of the influenza virus, potentially reducing the severity and duration of the flu.
Linalool: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate the body's response to the flu virus, reducing fever and discomfort associated with the condition.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in cineole, a compound that has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective against flu symptoms.
Buds: The buds of Melaleuca alternifolia contain a high concentration of cineole, which helps to reduce inflammation and combat flu viruses.
Stems: The stems of the plant are used to create essential oils that contain cineole and other compounds that help to soothe respiratory issues and reduce the severity of flu symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 kg of fresh leaves in the early morning when the dew is still present on them.
Steam distill the leaves for 3 hours at a temperature between 80-100°c to release the oil.
Collect the distilled oil and separate it from the water using a separation device.
Filter the oil through a 0.2 micron filter paper to remove any impurities and particles.
Store the final product in dark glass bottles with a tight-fitting lid to preserve its quality and shelf life.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils helps with flu because it contains active compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. These compounds work by reducing inflammation in the body, soothing a sore throat, and alleviating fever. Additionally, ginger's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe and recover from flu symptoms. remedy_constituents: Zingiberene: It has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the severity of flu symptoms such as congestion and body aches.
Gingerol: It exhibits antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help combat the flu virus and alleviate symptoms such as fever and cough.
Shogaol: It has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect the body from oxidative stress and inflammation caused by the flu virus.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of active compounds such as gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Leaves: Leaves are used to produce essential oils rich in sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to have decongestant and expectorant properties, helping to relieve flu symptoms.
Buds: Buds are used to create essential oils containing volatile oils that have antiviral and antibacterial properties, making them effective in fighting flu viruses and bacterial infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes for distillation after 9 to 12 months of growth.
Clean and dry the harvested rhizomes within 24 hours of collection to prevent mold.
Steam distill the dried rhizomes at 3 to 5 bars pressure for 2 to 4 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from water using a separator.
Filter and bottle the final product within 24 hours of distillation to preserve quality.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris essential oils helps with flu because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which inhibit the growth of influenza viruses and bacteria that can cause secondary infections. The oil's expectorant properties also help to relieve congestion and coughs associated with flu, while its anti-inflammatory properties reduce fever and body aches. Bornyl acetate: Possesses antiviral and expectorant properties, helping to relieve congestion and cough associated with the flu.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thyme leaves are the most commonly used part of the plant to make essential oils, as they contain the highest concentration of thymol, a natural antiseptic and antibacterial compound.
Stems: Thyme stems are used to make essential oils as they contain a significant amount of thymol and other bioactive compounds that help to combat flu and other respiratory infections.
Flowers: Thyme flowers are used to make essential oils, which have a more delicate and floral aroma compared to the leaf and stem oils, and are still valued for their antimicrobial properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the thymus vulgaris leaves by cutting them with scissors at dawn when they are at their highest oil content.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer at room temperature for 7 to 10 days.
Steam distill the dried thymus vulgaris leaves at 100 to 110 degrees celsius for 2 to 3 hours.
Collect the resulting essential oil in a separate container and filter it through a coffee filter.
Store the final thymus vulgaris essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a tight lid.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves at dawn when the dew is still present on the plant.
Steam distillation of the leaves is done at a temperature of 100 degrees celsius.
Distillation takes approximately 2 hours to separate the essential oil from the water.
The essential oil is separated from the water through decantation and filtration.
Store the rosmarinus officinalis essential oil in a cool dark place in a glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba essential oils helps with flu because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Seeds: The seeds of Ginkgo biloba are used because they contain bilobalide and ginkgolides, which have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that help combat flu viruses.

Barks: The barks of Ginkgo biloba are used because they contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce flu symptoms and prevent complications.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 ginkgo biloba leaves from mature trees in the early morning to ensure optimal oil content.
Steam distill the leaves in a distillation apparatus for 2 hours to release the oil molecules.
Collect the distilled oil and separate it from the water using a separation funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a 50-micron filter paper to remove impurities and particles.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum essential oils helps with flu because it possesses potent antiviral properties that inhibit the growth and replication of influenza viruses. remedy_constituents: Eugenol: It has antimicrobial properties, which help combat the influenza virus and reduce the severity of flu symptoms.
Cinnamaldehyde: It has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce the inflammation and fever associated with flu.
Linalool: It has immunomodulatory effects, which help boost the immune system and reduce the duration of flu symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used to make essential oils that can help reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues associated with the flu, thanks to their high concentration of eugenol and other volatile compounds.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes, or underground stems, of Cinnamomum verum contain a high amount of cinnamaldehyde and other compounds that can help combat the flu virus by reducing inflammation and fighting off infection.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature cinnamomum verum bark, cut into small pieces, and weigh 2 pounds for distillation.
Load the distillation apparatus with the prepared cinnamomum verum bark and add 5 liters of water.
Heat the distillation apparatus at 100-150 degrees fahrenheit for 2-3 hours to release the oil.
Collect the distillate and separate the essential oil from the hydrosol using a separating funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter and store it in a dark glass bottle at room temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils helps with flu because of its antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. It contains compounds like glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, which have been shown to have immunomodulatory effects, helping to boost the body's natural defenses against the flu virus. Additionally, its decongestant and expectorant properties can help relieve respiratory congestion and coughs associated with the flu. This makes Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oil a potential natural remedy for alleviating flu symptoms and promoting recovery.
remedy_constituents: Licoricidin: Licoricidin exhibits antiviral properties, helping to combat the flu virus and reduce its replication.
Licorisonide: Licorisonide has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of the flu such as fever, sore throat, and body aches.
Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to combat bacterial and viral infections, including the flu, and reduce inflammation in the body.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots are used due to their high glycyrrhizin content, which has anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties.
Leaves: Leaves are used due to their flavonoid and saponin content, which have immune-boosting and antiviral properties that help combat flu.
Roots: Roots are also used due to their antimicrobial properties, which help protect against flu-related infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots from a reputable supplier and store them in a dry location for later use.
Steam distill the dried roots for 3 hours to extract the essential oil using a steam distillation apparatus.
Collect the distilled oil and separate it from the water using a separatory funnel for 30 minutes.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter to remove any impurities and achieve a clear consistency.
Store the herbal glycyrrhiza glabra essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid and label it properly.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia essential oils helps with flu because it possesses potent immune-boosting properties. The oil contains compounds like alkylamides and caffeic acid, which have been shown to stimulate the production of white blood cells, helping the body fight off infections. Additionally, Echinacea angustifolia essential oils have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which can help reduce the severity of flu symptoms, such as fever, congestion, and cough. This natural remedy can provide relief and support the body's natural defenses against the flu virus.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce the severity of flu symptoms such as fever, headache, and muscle pain.
Alkylamides: Alkylamides in Echinacea angustifolia essential oils have immunomodulatory effects, which help boost the body's immune system to fight off the flu virus.
Cichoric Acid: Cichoric acid has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce the severity of flu symptoms and prevent secondary infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used to make essential oils for flu because they contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds, particularly echinacoside, which is believed to have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects.

Leaves: The leaves are used to make essential oils for flu because they are rich in flavonoids, such as quercetin, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help combat flu symptoms.

Flowers: The flowers are used to make essential oils for flu because they contain a range of bioactive compounds, including alkylamides and sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, helping to alleviate flu symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh echinacea angustifolia flowers and leaves in the early morning after the dew has evaporated.
Dry the plant material in a well-ventilated area using a dehydrator at 95 degrees fahrenheit.
Use a steam distillation apparatus to extract the essential oil from the dried plant material.
Collect the essential oil and separate it from the distilled water using a separatory funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter to remove any impurities and store it in a dark glass bottle.
